title: Add M5 anomaly roster and borrowed-shadow data lift
summary: Add the M5 anomaly roster owner surface and lift the reviewed M4 borrowed-shadow rule into the roster without changing M4 behavior or introducing bespoke content code paths.
acceptance: M5 anomaly roster rows are compiled immutable rule inputs with stable def ids DefIndex order roster version and validation basis
Mutable anomaly state remains in explicit sim-core owner stores and not in content defs UI projections or director state
Borrowed-shadow behavior is lifted as core.anomaly.borrowed_shadow.v1 without regressing the reviewed M4 scenario hashes or owner boundaries
Activation reads use bounded versioned candidates and stable Top-K ordering rather than scanning all lamps evidence residents or content defs
Focused tests cover roster validation stable ordering borrowed-shadow parity activation cap hits stale content basis rejection and structured reasons

history.details: Created by WM-0072 M5 planning DAG; remains proposed until dependencies are done.

Implemented M5 anomaly roster owner surface with borrowed-shadow data lift, versioned per-DefIndex activation lanes, stale content-basis rejection, focused m5-anomaly-roster tests, docs and report; required checks and pnpm quality pass; M4 vertical-slice hashes preserved.

changes_requested: High: borrowed-shadow compiled row validation does not protect the reviewed M4 rule basis. createM5BorrowedShadowAnomalyDefinition emits the correct M4 minActivationScore, evidence mask and non-combat mask, but loadCompiledRoster accepts a core.anomaly.borrowed_shadow.v1 row with minActivationScore=0, evidenceClassMask=0, nonCombatResolutionMask=0, or schemaVersion=2 and publishes it as owner state. validateDefinition only checks generic positive/range fields plus ruleComponent/activationPolicy/stateOwnerKind for borrowed shadow. This violates WM-0074 acceptance that the borrowed-shadow lift preserve M4 behavior/owner boundaries and that roster rows are validated immutable rule inputs. Also reject duplicate defIds across all roster rows; current validateRoster only detects duplicate borrowed-shadow ids and accepts duplicate future def ids, undermining stable def id/DefIndex ownership. Add focused regressions for tampered borrowed-shadow thresholds/masks/schema and all-def duplicate ids.

Repaired WM-0074 review findings: borrowed-shadow roster rows now require exact reviewed M4 schema, threshold, masks, component, policy and owner-kind basis; duplicate defIds across all roster rows are rejected; added regressions for tampered borrowed-shadow rows and duplicate future defs. Required checks and pnpm quality pass; M4 vertical-slice hashes preserved.

verified: No blocking findings. Verified borrowed-shadow roster rows now require exact reviewed M4 schema version, threshold, evidence mask, non-combat mask, rule component, activation policy and state owner kind before owner publish; duplicate defIds across all roster rows are rejected. Bounded per-DefIndex activation lanes, stable Top-K ordering, stale content-basis rejection, M4 36000 hashes, allowed scope and required gates are confirmed.

Reviewed M5 anomaly roster accepted after exact borrowed-shadow basis and duplicate defId repairs; integration gates passed: validate-handoff, taskctl validate/status, m5-anomaly-roster tests, M4 36000 scenario, git diff --check and pnpm quality. No protocol/save/package/M6 changes.

objective: Introduce the M5 anomaly roster owner contract and prove borrowed shadow can be rostered data without behavior drift.
risksAndFileOwnership: Risk: lifting borrowed shadow changes M4 evidence; owner must preserve M4 scenario command/content hashes or document a blocker.
Risk: roster becomes authority; mutable state must stay in sim-core owner stores.
Risk: activation scans content or world; candidate lanes and caps must be measured.
benchmarkImpact: Adds anomaly roster count activation candidate and cap-hit counters; baseline update waits for WM-0083.
reviewRouting: Simulation-engineer completes to reviewer; systems-architect must be consulted before changing roster schema or M4 borrowed-shadow semantics.
rollbackModel: Revert M5 roster and borrowed-shadow data-lift changes; keep WM-0073 validation and all M4 behavior intact.
ownerGate: Keep content defs immutable and non-authoritative
Use bounded activation lanes with stable Top-K ordering
Block on schema protocol save dependency UI authority or M6 scope

sparkEligibility.reason: Anomaly roster ownership and M4 behavior preservation touch deterministic simulation architecture.

review.summary: No blocking findings. Verified borrowed-shadow roster rows now require exact reviewed M4 schema version, threshold, evidence mask, non-combat mask, rule component, activation policy and state owner kind before owner publish; duplicate defIds across all roster rows are rejected. Bounded per-DefIndex activation lanes, stable Top-K ordering, stale content-basis rejection, M4 36000 hashes, allowed scope and required gates are confirmed.

integration.summary: Reviewed M5 anomaly roster accepted after exact borrowed-shadow basis and duplicate defId repairs; integration gates passed: validate-handoff, taskctl validate/status, m5-anomaly-roster tests, M4 36000 scenario, git diff --check and pnpm quality. No protocol/save/package/M6 changes.
